Politics.headline: White House navigates vaccine politics, midterm risks in CDC director search
Politics.detail: The Trump administration faces complicated political dynamics as it seeks someone to helm the key public health agency suffering a prolonged leadership vacuum. Whoever leads the CDC will need the approval of Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.

Politics.headline: Trump continues to target Robert Mueller after his death
Politics.detail: Despite his passing, Trump continues to attack Robert Mueller. Mueller was the former FBI Director who investigated Russia-Trump campaign ties.

Politics.headline: Senate rejects Trump-backed measure to ban trans athletes in female sports
Politics.detail: The Senate has rejected a measure backed by Trump that would have banned transgender athletes from participating in female sports. The bill faced strong opposition and failed to pass.

Politics.headline: Trump threatens to deploy ICE to airports as TSA shortages drive delays
Politics.detail: Amidst TSA staff shortages causing significant delays, Trump is threatening to deploy ICE agents to airports. He claims they would 'do security like no one has ever seen before'.

Politics.headline: Chávez celebrations halted nationwide as allegations reshape legacy
Politics.detail: Celebrations commemorating Chávez have been halted across the nation due to emerging allegations that are reshaping his legacy. The repercussions are forcing a reckoning within the union.

Finance & Economy.headline: China's fiscal policy to place greater emphasis on openness, shared benefits over next five years: finance minister
Finance & Economy.detail: China's Minister of Finance, Lan Fo'an, stated that the country's fiscal policy will focus on openness and shared benefits, aiming to allow global participation in China's development and strengthen international financial cooperation amidst a subdued global economy.

Finance & Economy.headline: Shockwave of War Is Rippling Through the Global Economy
Finance & Economy.detail: Business surveys are anticipated to show a decline in March, and the Reserve Bank of New Zealand Governor Anna Breman will discuss the inflation implications of the Middle East conflict, with economists expecting price growth to exceed the target band.

Finance & Economy.headline: China's New Chapter: Rebalancing and Unleashing Market Forces
Finance & Economy.detail: China's policymakers are addressing economic challenges by adopting a more expansionary fiscal policy, reducing over-investment, and prioritizing increased consumption in their 15th Five-Year Plan to drive economic growth and reduce external imbalances.

Finance & Economy.headline: UK Should Consider Temporary Cap on Energy Profits, Adviser Says
Finance & Economy.detail: An advisor suggests the UK should consider a temporary cap on energy profits.

Finance & Economy.headline: US open: Stocks lower as oil surges on fresh Middle East escalation.
Finance & Economy.detail: US Stocks are lower as oil prices surge due to fresh escalations in the Middle East.

Technology.headline: Reddit Could Soon Require Face ID to Prove You're Not a Bot
Technology.detail: Reddit CEO Steve Huffman said Face ID, Touch ID, and other forms of passkey technology were the 'most lightweight way' to ensure all Reddit users were human.

Technology.headline: Nintendo Switch 2 Could Soon Let Users Replace Their Own Batteries
Technology.detail: The new feature will be rolled out gradually for the Switch 2's detachable Joy-Con controllers sold in the EU, Nikkei Asia reports, ahead of the bloc's new rules coming into force.

Technology.headline: Major Publisher Drops Horror Novel 'Shy Girl' Amid AI Allegations
Technology.detail: Shy Girl sold just under 2,000 copies in the UK and has been reviewed almost 5,000 times on Goodreads, and has now been pulled by its publishers from Amazon and its website.

Technology.headline: How to AirDrop on an Android phone (and the few models that can actually do it)
Technology.detail: A guide on how to use Android's Nearby Share feature, which functions similarly to Apple's AirDrop, for quick file sharing.

Technology.headline: Logitech Takes Aim at Bulky Boardroom Gear With AI Cameras
Technology.detail: Logitech is introducing new AI-powered cameras designed to streamline and improve the meeting room experience, challenging traditional bulky hardware setups.

Science.headline: Weight Loss Drug Ozempic Cuts Depression, Anxiety, and Addiction Risk
Science.detail: GLP-1 medications like semaglutide (Ozempic) may offer unexpected mental health benefits alongside weight loss, according to new research.

Science.headline: Beavers Are Turning Rivers Into Powerful Carbon Sinks
Science.detail: New research suggests beavers could play a powerful role in fighting climate change by building dams and transforming streams into wetlands, which dramatically reshape how carbon moves.

Science.headline: Mosquitoes get the 'I'm full' signal from their butts, not their brains
Science.detail: Mosquitoes know when they're full thanks to signals from their abdomen, not their brain, according to recent findings.

Science.headline: NASA's Hubble Accidentally Caught a Comet Breaking Apart in Real Time
Science.detail: In a lucky cosmic accident, the Hubble Space Telescope captured a comet, C/2025 K1 (ATLAS), breaking apart in real time, an event astronomers have long sought to observe.

Science.headline: Molecule in python blood could pave way for new obesity drugs, scientists say
Science.detail: Scientists have identified a molecule in python blood that could lead to new treatments for obesity.

Health.headline: WHO says 64 killed in drone attack on hospital in East Darfur, Sudan
Health.detail: A drone attack on al-Dhaein Teaching Hospital in East Darfur, Sudan, resulted in the deaths of at least 64 people, including 13 children, a doctor, and two nurses, according to the World Health Organization. The attack has rendered the hospital non-functional, and the WHO has reported over 2,000 deaths in attacks on health facilities since the conflict in Sudan began.

Health.headline: WHO Chief warns Middle East war has reached perilous stage with nuclear‑site strikes
Health.detail: The Director-General of the World Health Organization (WHO) warned that the war in the Middle East has reached a dangerous point, citing reported strikes on the Natanz Enrichment Complex in Iran and the Israeli city of Dimona, which is home to a nuclear facility. The WHO has provided training to staff and UN personnel to prepare for public health threats in the event of a nuclear incident and is calling for military restraint.

Health.headline: New Pill Cuts “Bad” Cholesterol by 60% in Major Trial
Health.detail: A new oral pill, enlicitide, has demonstrated the ability to reduce LDL ("bad") cholesterol by approximately 60% in a significant clinical trial, performing similarly to injectable therapies. This could provide a more accessible option for patients, overcoming barriers associated with injectable treatments.

Health.headline: Global warming could increase inactivity and premature death
Health.detail: Rising global temperatures are projected to increase physical inactivity, especially in lower-income countries, potentially leading to an increase of 0.47–0.70 million additional deaths annually and significant productivity losses by 2050. This is expected to worsen global health and economic inequalities.

Health.headline: More parents are refusing routine newborn preventative care, study finds
Health.detail: A recent study indicates an increasing number of parents are declining routine preventative care for their newborns. Doctors are concerned that skepticism, influenced by anti-science sentiments and mistrust, is expanding beyond vaccines to encompass other established and routine care practices.

Sports.headline: De Sousa soars to long jump title in Kujawy Pomorze
Sports.detail: Portugal's Agate De Sousa won gold in the long jump final at the World Athletics Indoor Championships Kujawy Pomorze 26 with a jump of 6.92m, ahead of Italy's Larissa Iapichino and Colombia's Natalia Linares. Iapichino took silver with 6.87m, and Linares won bronze with 6.80m.

Sports.headline: Walsh makes history in Kujawy Pomorze with fourth world indoor shot put title
Sports.detail: New Zealand's Tom Walsh won his fourth world indoor shot put title at the World Athletics Indoor Championships Kujawy Pomorze 26, earning his seventh world indoor medal, a record for any male athlete. Walsh's best throw was a season's best of 21.82m.

Sports.headline: EAGLE ROCK! West Coast's 47-point turnaround stuns AFL to snap gruesome losing streak
Sports.detail: The West Coast Eagles secured their first win of 2026, overcoming a five-goal deficit to defeat North Melbourne by 17 points. This victory ends their 14-game losing streak.

Sports.headline: New Port coach fearing the worst for superstar skipper as first win soured, Bombers crisis deepens
Sports.detail: Josh Carr achieved his first win as Port Adelaide coach, defeating Essendon by 63 points; however, the victory was overshadowed by a knee injury to Essendon defender Mason Redman. Redman left the game with a likely left meniscus tear, potentially sidelining him for at least a month.

Sports.headline: SA Clinch 19-Run Win Over New Zealand In 4th T20I, Take Series To Decider
Sports.detail: South Africa beat New Zealand by 19 runs in the fourth T20I to level the series at 2-2. The deciding match will be at Christchurch on Wednesday, March 25, 2026.

Entertainment.headline: Michael B. Jordan wins best actor for 'Sinners,' taking home his first Oscar in popular victory.
Entertainment.detail: Michael B. Jordan won the best actor Oscar for his role in 'Sinners'. This is his first Oscar win.

Entertainment.headline: Jessie Buckley wins the best actress Oscar for tear-jerking 'Hamnet' performance.
Entertainment.detail: Jessie Buckley received the best actress Oscar for her performance in 'Hamnet'. The awards were held at Dolby Theatre.

Entertainment.headline: Chuck Norris dies at age 86
Entertainment.detail: Action star Chuck Norris has passed away at the age of 86, according to his family.

Entertainment.headline: Taylor Frankie Paul Temporarily Loses Custody of Son With Dakota Mortenson After Brutal Fight Video
Entertainment.detail: Taylor Frankie Paul temporarily lost custody of her son with Dakota Mortenson after he was granted a protective order on Friday.

Entertainment.headline: Bodycam Video From Timberlake's 2024 Arrest
Entertainment.detail: Nearly two years after his DWI arrest, police bodycam footage from Timberlake's encounter with police was released to the public.

Education.headline: Odisha Joint Entrance Examination 2026 Registration Deadline Extended, Apply Here
Education.detail: The registration deadline for the Odisha Joint Entrance Examination 2026 has been extended, providing additional time for interested candidates to apply.

Education.headline: IIT Bombay Releases JAM 2026 Counselling Schedule And Guidelines
Education.detail: IIT Bombay has announced the counselling schedule and guidelines for the Joint Admission Test for Masters (JAM) 2026, providing important information for prospective students.

Education.headline: How Students Can View Rajasthan Board 10th Result On DigiLocker, Explained
Education.detail: An article explains the process for students to access and view their Rajasthan Board 10th result on DigiLocker.

Education.headline: IGNOU June 2026 Term End Examination: Form Submission Starts, Exams Scheduled From June 1
Education.detail: The form submission process has commenced for the IGNOU June 2026 Term End Examination, with exams scheduled to begin on June 1st.

Education.headline: Delhi DSSSB Recruitment 2026: Registration Ends Soon For 911 Vacancies, Apply Here
Education.detail: The registration window for the Delhi Subordinate Services Selection Board (DSSSB) Recruitment 2026, offering 911 vacancies, is closing soon.

Lifestyle.headline: Cortina d'Ampezzo mixes Olympic legacy with Alpine glamour
Lifestyle.detail: Cortina d'Ampezzo, Italy, blends its rich Olympic history with stunning Alpine scenery, offering a unique lifestyle destination.

Lifestyle.headline: 6 Ways to Improve Your Coachability and Reach Your Potential Faster
Lifestyle.detail: Improving coachability can significantly accelerate personal and professional development by enhancing receptiveness to feedback and guidance.

Lifestyle.headline: Baby in Quarantine Before Heart Surgery Turns 1-Tears at What Family Does
Lifestyle.detail: A baby who was quarantined before heart surgery celebrates their first birthday, with the family creating a heartwarming celebration.

Lifestyle.headline: The Hidden Downsides of Terracotta Planters Every Gardener Should Know
Lifestyle.detail: Terracotta planters, while aesthetically pleasing, have hidden drawbacks that gardeners should be aware of, such as moisture absorption and potential cracking.

Lifestyle.headline: Interior Designer, 61, Admits He Doesn't Have Money to Last Him as Lavish Lifestyle Is 'Long Gone': 'I've Got to Die at 81'
Lifestyle.detail: Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen, a 61-year-old interior designer, confesses that his past extravagant spending habits have led to a precarious financial situation.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Editorial | Is Dallas prepared to make tough budget choices?
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Dallas Morning News Editorial Board discusses whether Dallas is ready to make difficult decisions regarding its budget.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The case against the Senate filibuster no Republican can make
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Jason Willick argues that ending the 60-vote tradition in the Senate would put Congress, not the president, in the U.S. politics driver's seat.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: It's time for the UN to formally recognise the transatlantic slavery trade as a crime against humanity
Opinion/Editorial.detail: John Dramani Mahama argues for the United Nations to formally recognize the transatlantic slavery trade as a crime against humanity.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Middle East: Iran Targets Israeli Town That Hosts Nuclear Facility
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Reports indicate an Iranian missile struck Dimona, an Israeli town housing a nuclear facility, wounding a 12-year-old boy and over 30 others.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on meningitis in Kent: we must not take public health systems for granted
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Guardian's editorial board emphasizes the importance of maintaining public health systems, referencing a meningitis outbreak in Kent.

World.headline: Trump threatens attacks on Iranian power plants if Tehran fails to open the Strait of Hormuz
World.detail: President Donald Trump warned the U.S. will obliterate Iran's power plants if Tehran fails to fully open the Strait of Hormuz within 48 hours, prompting Iran to respond with threats of its own, saying all US energy infrastructure in the region will be targeted if it is attacked. The Strait of Hormuz connects the Persian Gulf to the Indian Ocean and is a critical pathway for the world's flow of oil.

World.headline: About 200 injured in Iranian missile strikes near nuclear facility in Israel
World.detail: Iranian missiles struck two communities in southern Israel, leaving buildings shattered and dozens injured in dual attacks not far from Israel's main nuclear research center. Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said that the missile attacks are evidence that Iran is a threat to the entire world.

World.headline: WHO says attack on Sudan hospital killed 64, including 13 children
World.detail: The World Health Organization (WHO) reported that an attack on a hospital in Sudan resulted in the deaths of 64 people, including 13 children. The attack, believed to be an army drone strike on a health facility in East Darfur, also wounded 89 more people, with victims including children and medical personnel.

World.headline: Casualties as Israeli settlers set fire to homes and cars in West Bank
World.detail: Israeli settlers set fire to homes and cars near Jenin amid reports of widespread violence across the occupied territory. This incident follows a series of attacks and rising tensions in the region.

World.headline: Sri Lanka raises fuel prices by 25% as war bites
World.detail: Due to the ongoing war and its impact on global supply chains, Sri Lanka has increased fuel prices by 25%. The country is also facing severe economic challenges, including fuel shortages and rising inflation.

Zimbabwe.headline: Work starts on landmark RGM Airport Expressway
Zimbabwe.detail: Construction has commenced on the Robert Gabriel Mugabe (RGM) Airport Expressway, a significant infrastructure project expected to improve connectivity and ease traffic flow to and from the airport.

Zimbabwe.headline: VP pushes manufacturers to tap into Comesa, Sadc markets
Zimbabwe.detail: Vice President is encouraging local manufacturers to increase their presence in the Common Market for Eastern and Southern Africa (Comesa) and Southern African Development Community (SADC) markets, promoting regional trade and economic growth.

Zimbabwe.headline: Women's University honours founders
Zimbabwe.detail: The Women's University in Africa (WUA) is celebrating and recognizing the contributions of its founders, highlighting their role in establishing an institution dedicated to female education and empowerment.

Zimbabwe.headline: Nation prepares for solemn triple burial of national heroes
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe is preparing for the burial of three national heroes, with the event expected to be a solemn occasion to honor their contributions to the country.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zim evacuates nationals from Iran, monitors Middle East
Zimbabwe.detail: In light of escalating tensions in the Middle East, Zimbabwe has evacuated its citizens from Iran and is closely monitoring the situation to ensure the safety of its nationals in the region.
